findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[expose\].*(append|initialSegmentFamily)|toPath_append|endpoint_append|initialSegmentFamily_apply|endpoint_initialSegmentFamily|toPath_initialSegmentFamily" code/TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/BasedPath.lean` shows exposed defs at lines 138, 239, 272 and characteristic lemmas at 142, 146, 251, 255, 260, 284, 288, 295.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from these operation definitions and restate exported characteristic lemmas with explicit casts/proofs, adding any missing `*_def` lemma only if downstream code really needs it.
findings.issue: `BasedPath.append` and both `initialSegmentFamily` definitions remain `@[expose]`, making their implementations part of the public reduction surface despite having public endpoint/toPath/apply/zero/one/continuity lemmas.

summary: The characteristic API gaps for initial segments and append are fixed, but several implementation bodies are still exposed even though downstream users can be served by the new API lemmas.
